title: Disposable types should declare finalizers
description: Disposable types should declare finalizersAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalmaintainabilityCode SmellWhy is this an issue?More InfoThis rule raises an issue when a disposable type contains fields of the following types and does not implement a finalizer:System.IntPtrSystem.UIntPtrSystem.Runtime.InteropService.HandleRefNoncompliant code exampleusing System; using System.Runtime.InteropServices; namespace MyLibrary { public class Foo : IDisposable // Noncompliant: Doesn't have a finalizer { private IntPtr myResource; private bool disposed = false; protected virtual void Dispose(bool disposing) { if (!disposed) { // Dispose of resources held by this instance. FreeResource(myResource); disposed = true; // Suppress finalization of this disposed instance. if (disposing) { GC.SuppressFinal
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Detect IDisposable classes with unmanaged resources (IntPtr, Marshal, SafeHandle) but no finalizer
logic.triggering_conditions: Implements IDisposable AND uses unmanaged resources AND missing finalizer (~ClassName)
logic.reasoning: Classes that implement IDisposable and hold unmanaged resources must have finalizers to ensure resources are released even if Dispose() is not called. Without a finalizer, unmanaged resources can leak.

should_trigger: public class MyClass : IDisposable { private IntPtr handle; public void Dispose() { } }
should_not_trigger: public class MyClass : IDisposable { ~MyClass() { } private IntPtr handle; }
